related_questions.snippet: Proceedings in Small Claims Court are less formal than in other civil courts. The Plaintiff's side of the case will be presented first. The Plaintiff should offer any documents which support the plaintiff's claim and present any witnesses at this time.
related_questions.title: Small Claims Procures & How to Start the Suit | Delta County, TX

text_blocks.snippet: To file a small claims case, determine the correct local courthouse, complete the required claim form (often called a Statement or Affidavit of Claim), pay the filing fee, and formally serve the defendant.

list.snippet: Limits: Small claims courts handle disputes below a specific monetary cap (e.g., $10,000 in New York, $12,500 in California).

Location:
File your case in the district or county court where the defendant lives, works, or where the dispute originated.
Gather all relevant evidence, contracts, and receipts.

Obtain the required forms online or from your local court clerk’s office
. Ensure you have the exact legal name and address of the person or business you are suing.
Sign the forms in front of a court clerk or a notary public, if required.
Filing fees typically range from $15 to $100, depending on the amount claimed and your local jurisdiction.

If you cannot afford the fee, ask the clerk for a
fee waiver form.
The defendant must be formally notified of the lawsuit (served) with the court papers by a specific deadline.

Depending on your court, you may use certified mail, the sheriff's department, or a professional process server. You cannot serve the papers yourself
.
After service is complete, you must file a Proof of Service form with the court clerk.

text_blocks.snippet: For a visual guide on completing the correct forms and naming the right defendants in Los Angeles County (which applies to many state systems):

If you tell me your city or state, I can find the exact claim forms, filing fees, and monetary limits
for your local court.
